mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications that require efficient organization and searching of vast amounts of unstructured data. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes for deployment and the complexity of managing a distributed system.

steps.title: Add the Milvus Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Milvus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Milvus using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Milvus Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Milvus CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Milvus to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/crds/milvus.io_milvusclusters.yaml

```

> This command installs the Milvus CRDs.

steps.title: Install Milvus using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Milvus in the specified namespace using Helm, which simplifies the deployment process.

```bash

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --create-namespace --version 2.5.0

```

> Installs Milvus version 2.5.0 in the 'milvus' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an example Milvus cluster resource that defines the desired state of your Milvus deployment.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus-cluster
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: v2.5.0
EOF

```

> This command creates a Milvus cluster resource.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus-cluster
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: v2.5.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Milvus Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Milvus cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get milvusclusters -n milvus

```

> This command lists the Milvus clusters in the 'milvus' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Milvus deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are made.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> Uninstalls the Milvus Helm release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backups of your data. This step will upgrade the Milvus installation to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --version 2.6.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Drains the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades Milvus to version 2.6.0.
> Uncordons the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MilvusCluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Milvus cluster resource does not exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Milvus cluster resource is created with the correct name and namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Milvus pods using 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the Milvus pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resources available in your Kubernetes cluster or adjust the resource requests in the Milvus configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD validation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The installed CRDs may be outdated or incompatible with the current Milvus version.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using the command provided in the installation steps.

**Affected versions:** <= 2.5

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old RESTful API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your application code to use the new API endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus cluster listed with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get milvusclusters -n milvus'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: milvus.io/v1
kind: MilvusCluster
metadata:
  name: milvus-cluster
  namespace: milvus
spec:
  mode: cluster
  version: v2.5.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
